evals.prompt: I have ID Capture working in my .NET iOS app (see IntegratedViewController.cs). I'm scanning US driver's licenses and I need to detect forged AAMVA barcodes and reject them, and also flag licenses where the front and back data don't match. When a license is captured, I want to read the verification result so I can show whether the barcode looked authentic. How do I do this in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill enables RejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true and RejectInconsistentData = true on the IdCaptureSettings (NO standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier / DataConsistencyVerifier class, which don't exist on .NET), and reads capturedId.VerificationResult in OnIdCaptured: AamvaBarcodeVerification.Status (AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus.Authentic/LikelyForged/Forged) and DataConsistency.AllChecksPassed. It also notes that a confirmed forgery surfaces via OnIdRejected with RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode (and InconsistentData). The rest of the integration is preserved.

assertions.text: RejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true is set on the IdCaptureSettings
RejectInconsistentData = true is set on the IdCaptureSettings
No standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier class is instantiated or referenced (these do not exist on .NET; verification is settings-driven)
The AAMVA outcome is read via VerificationResult.AamvaBarcodeVerification and its Status (AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus.Authentic / LikelyForged / Forged)
The data-consistency outcome is read via VerificationResult.DataConsistency (e.g. AllChecksPassed)
It explains that hard failures surface via OnIdRejected with RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode (and/or InconsistentData)
The existing integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView, overlay, accepted documents, scanner, OnIdCaptured/OnIdRejected, ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET iOS app I only care about the PDF417 barcode on the back of US driver's licenses (see IntegratedViewController.cs) — I don't need to scan the front. I also want to pull the document images (the cropped document and the holder's face photo) out of the result so I can display them. How do I configure this in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill switches the scanner to a SingleSideScanner with only the barcode zone enabled (new SingleSideScanner(barcode: true, machineReadableZone: false, visualInspectionZone: false)) wrapped in IdCaptureScanner, narrows AcceptedDocuments to new DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.Us), and reads capturedId.Images (Face / GetCroppedDocument(IdSide)) — noting the images are UIImage? on iOS (NOT Android.Graphics.Bitmap). It can also read AAMVA fields from capturedId.Barcode. The rest of the integration is preserved.

assertions.text: The scanner is changed to a SingleSideScanner with the barcode zone enabled and the MRZ/VIZ zones disabled (e.g. new SingleSideScanner(barcode: true, machineReadableZone: false, visualInspectionZone: false))
The SingleSideScanner is wrapped in new IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: ..., mobileDocument: null) and assigned to settings.Scanner
AcceptedDocuments is narrowed to a US driver's license (new DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.Us))
Document images are read from capturedId.Images (e.g. Face and/or GetCroppedDocument(IdSide.Front))
The images are treated as UIImage? (iOS), NOT Android.Graphics.Bitmap
No FullDocumentScanner is used (the user only wants the back barcode), and no front-only zones are enabled
The existing integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView, overlay, OnIdCaptured/OnIdRejected, lifecycle) is preserved
